questions.text: What is the primary purpose of staking in a Proof-of-Stake blockchain?

options.text: To secure the network by aligning validators' economic incentives with honest behavior.
To create new tokens out of thin air.
To make tokens impossible to trade.
To replace smart contracts.

questions.explanation: Staking locks tokens as collateral for validators, ensuring they have "skin in the game." If they misbehave, their stake is slashed.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Staking locks tokens as collateral for validators, ensuring they have "skin in the game." If they misbehave, their stake is slashed.

questions.text: What is a liquid staking derivative (like stMSTC)?

options.text: A token representing staked MSTC that can be used in DeFi while still earning staking rewards.
A stablecoin pegged to the value of MSTC.
A token that burns your staked MSTC.
A governance token for the MST blockchain.

questions.explanation: Liquid staking tokens let you earn staking rewards while also using the derivative in lending, liquidity pools, or yield farms - composability at its best.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Liquid staking tokens let you earn staking rewards while also using the derivative in lending, liquidity pools, or yield farms - composability at its best.

questions.text: In yield farming, what is the typical first step to start earning?

options.text: Provide liquidity to a DEX pool (or deposit into a lending protocol).
Borrow tokens without any collateral.
Sell all tokens for a stablecoin.
Burn tokens to reduce supply.

questions.explanation: Yield farming begins with providing capital to a protocol - either as a lender, liquidity provider, or staker. That earns base yield, which can then be further optimized.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Yield farming begins with providing capital to a protocol - either as a lender, liquidity provider, or staker. That earns base yield, which can then be further optimized.

questions.text: What is "harvesting" in the context of yield farming?

options.text: Claiming accumulated reward tokens and usually reinvesting them to compound.
Destroying old crops.
Removing liquidity from a pool.
Staking LP tokens in a farm.

questions.explanation: Harvesting is the act of collecting earned rewards. Most farmers then compound - selling a portion and reinvesting to grow their position.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Harvesting is the act of collecting earned rewards. Most farmers then compound - selling a portion and reinvesting to grow their position.

questions.text: Which of the following is a risk specific to providing liquidity in an AMM for yield farming?

options.text: Impermanent loss.
The pool running out of tokens entirely.
The DEX being shut down by the government.
The LP tokens becoming worthless due to a bug.

questions.explanation: Impermanent loss occurs when the price ratio of the paired tokens changes, reducing the LP's value relative to simply holding them.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Impermanent loss occurs when the price ratio of the paired tokens changes, reducing the LP's value relative to simply holding them.

questions.text: What is the primary benefit of veTokenomics for a protocol?

options.text: It aligns long-term incentives by giving more rewards and voting power to users who lock tokens.
It makes tokens trade faster.
It eliminates the need for governance.
It increases the total supply of tokens.

questions.explanation: veTokenomics ensures that the most influence and highest rewards go to those who commit to the protocol long-term, reducing mercenary behavior.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

veTokenomics ensures that the most influence and highest rewards go to those who commit to the protocol long-term, reducing mercenary behavior.

questions.text: How does a lower gas fee (like MST's) directly benefit a yield farmer?

options.text: It allows more frequent compounding (harvesting and reinvesting) without fees eating into profits.
It guarantees higher APY from the farm.
It removes impermanent loss.
It makes all reward tokens stablecoins.

questions.explanation: Low gas means you can harvest daily or even hourly, capturing compound interest fully. On high-fee chains, frequent harvesting is unprofitable.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Low gas means you can harvest daily or even hourly, capturing compound interest fully. On high-fee chains, frequent harvesting is unprofitable.

questions.text: What does "APY" stand for and why is it used in DeFi?

options.text: Annual Percentage Yield - it reflects the compounded return over a year.
Average Payout Yield - the mean daily return.
Automated Protocol Yield - a technical term for smart contract earnings.
All-time Profit Yearly - total profit since inception.

questions.explanation: APY includes the effect of compounding, giving a more accurate picture of yearly returns than APR (simple interest).
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

APY includes the effect of compounding, giving a more accurate picture of yearly returns than APR (simple interest).

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a typical risk in yield farming?

options.text: Impermanent loss.
Smart contract vulnerability.
Reward token price crash.
Guaranteed principal protection by the protocol.

questions.explanation: There is no guarantee of principal in yield farming. You can lose money due to price movements, bugs, or exploits. Always assess risk.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: D

There is no guarantee of principal in yield farming. You can lose money due to price movements, bugs, or exploits. Always assess risk.

questions.text: Why might a protocol distribute governance tokens to yield farmers?

options.text: To bootstrap liquidity and distribute ownership to active users.
To increase gas fees for the network.
To make the tokens less valuable.
To prevent users from trading.

questions.explanation: Liquidity mining (distributing tokens to users) is a common strategy to attract deposits and decentralize governance.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Liquidity mining (distributing tokens to users) is a common strategy to attract deposits and decentralize governance.
